title: C57 mouse stereotaxic injection

steps: Anesthetize the mouse in an isoflurane induction chamber until loss of righting reflex is observed.
Transfer the animal to a stereotaxic frame (Kopf Instruments) equipped with a nose cone designed for mice to maintain anesthesia during the procedure.
Maintain anesthesia with isoflurane throughout the surgery, continuously monitoring the breathing rate. Adjust isoflurane levels as needed to maintain stable anesthesia.
Place the animal on a heating pad to maintain core body temperature.
Immediately before surgery, administer ketoprofen (5 mg/kg, SC) and buprenorphine-XR (3.25 mg/kg, SC) for systemic analgesia.
Disinfect the surgical site by applying chlorhexidine scrub and chlorhexidine solution alternately three times.
Inject 50-100 μL of 0.25% bupivacaine solution (in sterile saline) subcutaneously over the skull to provide local analgesia and maintain scalp moisture.
Using a sterile scalpel, make a midline incision over the skull.
Perform all surgical steps under sterile conditions, using autoclave-sterilized surgical tools and aseptic technique.
Perform stereotaxic injections using a 10 μL Hamilton syringe (Hamilton #1701) fitted with a 33-gauge needle, according to the planned coordinates.
After injections, gently release the animal from the stereotaxic ear bars before closing the incision to reduce skin tension.
Close the incision using non-absorbable nylon sutures (Ethicon #NW3353), ensuring proper alignment of the wound edges.
Allow the animal to recover on a heating pad and closely monitor until fully ambulatory.
Continue to monitor animals regularly for the next several days for signs of pain, distress, or surgical complications, providing supportive care as needed.

Before beginning the procedure, ensure that all experimental steps have been reviewed and approved under the appropriate institutional protocols, and that all personnel involved are properly trained and compliant with institutional regulations.
